title: p-Nilpotent Maximal Subgroups in Finite Groups
publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC
abstract:
Abstract
          Let p be a prime number and suppose that every maximal subgroup of a finite group is either p-nilpotent or has prime index. Such a group need not be p-solvable, and we study its structure by proving that only one nonabelian simple group of order divisible by p, which belongs to the family 
              
                $$\textrm{PSL}_n(q)$$
                
                  
                    
                      PSL
                      n
                    
                    
                      (
                      q
                      )
                    
                  
                
              
            , can be involved in it. For 
              
                $$p=2$$
                
                  
                    p
                    =
                    2
                  
                
              
            , we specify more, and in fact, such a simple group must be isomorphic to 
              
                $$\textrm{PSL}_2({r^a})$$
                
                  
                    
                      PSL
                      2
                    
                    
                      (
                      
                        r
                        a
                      
                      )
                    
                  
                
              
             for certain values of the prime r and the parameter a.
